skill.name: api-security-best-practices
skill.description: Implement secure API design patterns including authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limiting, and protection against common API vulnerabilities
skill.summary: Guide for implementing secure REST, GraphQL, and WebSocket APIs with authentication, input validation, rate limiting, and OWASP Top 10 protection
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate educational skill about API security best practices. All 117 static findings are false positives: the backtick patterns are markdown code formatting, environment variable access demonstrates proper secret management, URLs are documentation links, and heuristic alerts fire because the skill comprehensively covers security topics together. No actual security risks detected.

content.value_statement: Build secure APIs that protect against authentication bypass, injection attacks, and DDoS by implementing JWT authentication, input validation, rate limiting, and OWASP-recommended security patterns.
actual_capabilities: Guide implementation of JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens
Show how to prevent SQL injection and XSS through input validation
Demonstrate rate limiting and DDoS protection strategies
Provide security checklist based on OWASP API Top 10
Show secure error handling that prevents information leakage
limitations: Does not provide working code that can be executed directly
Does not test or audit existing APIs for vulnerabilities
Does not generate security certificates or keys
Skill content is static reference material, not an interactive security tool

use_cases.title: Secure New API Development
use_cases.description: Use this skill to guide secure design when building new REST, GraphQL, or WebSocket APIs from scratch

use_cases.title: Harden Existing APIs
use_cases.description: Review and improve security of existing APIs by implementing authentication, rate limiting, and input validation

use_cases.title: Security Audit Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare APIs for security audits by following OWASP Top 10 guidelines and implementing recommended protections

prompt_templates.title: Implement JWT Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me implement JWT authentication for my API. I need login, token verification middleware, and token refresh endpoints.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants secure auth for their API

prompt_templates.title: Prevent SQL Injection
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to prevent SQL injection in my Node.js API. Include parameterized query examples and input validation.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to secure database queries

prompt_templates.title: Add Rate Limiting
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement rate limiting for my Express API. I want different limits for regular users versus authentication endpoints.

prompt_templates.title: OWASP API Security Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my API against the OWASP API Security Top 10. What vulnerabilities should I check for and how do I fix them?

output_examples.input: How do I implement secure JWT authentication?
output_examples.output: Key implementation steps: 1) Use strong 256-bit JWT secrets from environment variables, 2) Set short expiration (1 hour) for access tokens, 3) Implement refresh tokens stored in database, 4) Verify issuer and audience claims, 5) Use HTTPS only, 6) Implement token blacklist for logout. Example code shows login endpoint with bcrypt password verification and JWT signing.

output_examples.input: What rate limiting strategy should I use?
output_examples.output: Implement tiered rate limiting: 1) General API limit (100 requests per 15 minutes), 2) Stricter limit for auth endpoints (5 attempts per 15 minutes), 3) Per-user limits based on subscription tier, 4) Use Redis for distributed rate limiting, 5) Return proper rate limit headers. Example uses express-rate-limit with Redis store.

output_examples.input: How do I prevent SQL injection?
output_examples.output: Never concatenate user input into SQL queries. Use: 1) Parameterized queries or ORM (Prisma, Sequelize), 2) Input validation with Zod or Joi, 3) Allowlists for data types, 4) Sanitize HTML for XSS with DOMPurify. Example shows vulnerable string concatenation vs safe parameterized query.

best_practices: Always use HTTPS and enforce TLS for all API traffic
Validate and sanitize all user input before processing
Implement defense in depth with multiple security layers
anti_patterns: Storing sensitive data in JWT payload (not encrypted)
Exposing detailed error messages in production

faq.question: Does this skill test my API for vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and code examples for implementing security. Use dedicated security testing tools for vulnerability scanning.

faq.question: What authentication methods does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers JWT (JSON Web Tokens), OAuth 2.0 concepts, API keys, and session-based authentication with bcrypt password hashing.

faq.question: Is this skill only for Node.js APIs?
faq.answer: The examples use Node.js/Express, but security principles apply to any framework or language. Concepts are language-agnostic.

faq.question: Does this skill help with compliance?
faq.answer: It covers security controls aligned with OWASP Top 10 and common best practices, which support compliance efforts but does not guarantee compliance.

faq.question: Can I use this for GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes, the authentication and input validation concepts apply to GraphQL. Additional GraphQL-specific topics like query depth limiting are not covered.

faq.question: How often should I update my API security?
faq.answer: Review security regularly, update dependencies frequently, monitor for new vulnerabilities, and conduct periodic penetration testing.
